reforms.description: Slovakia plans to increase R&D business sector funding from 0.4% to 1.3% of GDP, aiming to reach the OECD average level, which is expected to enhance innovation capacity and competitiveness.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D funding is likely to stimulate innovation and productivity growth in the business sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has outlined plans to increase R&D funding significantly.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a substantial increase in public investment in R&D, which is critical for enhancing Slovakia's innovation ecosystem and aligning with OECD standards.
reforms.source_quote: R&D business sector funding is increased from 0.4% to 1.3%, reaching the OECD average level.

reforms.description: The Slovak Republic plans to increase government support for R&D, which is expected to enhance innovation capacity and economic growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D funding is likely to stimulate innovation, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to increase support for R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing R&D funding, which is crucial for fostering innovation in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to increase spending on R&D to boost innovation.

reforms.description: The government has strengthened tax incentives to stimulate research and innovation initiatives, which were previously very limited. This aims to boost private investment in knowledge and enhance the overall innovation capacity in Slovakia.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing tax incentives is expected to increase private sector investment in R&D, leading to higher innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has taken steps to strengthen tax incentives for research and innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in policy aimed at enhancing the innovation landscape in Slovakia, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government has strengthened tax incentives to stimulate research and innovation initiatives, which were previously very limited.

reforms.description: The Slovak Republic has reformed its R&D tax credit to make it refundable, allowing 100% of R&D spending to be deducted from the tax base, an increase from 25%. Additionally, a patent box was introduced to exempt revenues from selected intangible assets from taxation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform incentivizes private sector investment in R&D, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The R&D tax credit reform was enacted and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the tax treatment of R&D, likely to enhance the innovation landscape in Slovakia.
reforms.source_quote: Reform the R&D tax credit to make it refundable. Promote 100% of R&D spending can now be deducted from the tax base, increased from 25%.

reforms.description: The Slovak government has implemented a facilitated procedure for hiring skilled workers from non-EU/EEA countries, aimed at addressing labor shortages in specific occupations. This includes exemptions from labor market tests for jobs in areas with low unemployment and a streamlined process for issuing work permits.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating the recruitment of skilled workers, the reform aims to enhance the labor supply, which is crucial for innovation and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The facilitated procedure for hiring skilled workers has been in place since May 2018.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a significant issue in the labor market by improving the hiring process for skilled workers, which is essential for meeting the demands of a changing economy.
reforms.source_quote: Since May 2018 a facilitated procedure - including a labour market test exemption - is in place for occupations in shortage in districts where unemployment is below 5%.

reforms.description: The Slovak government has increased the tax-deductible proportion of R&D expenditure for companies from 25% to 100% to stimulate private-sector research and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform is expected to boost private investment in R&D by making it more financially viable for firms to engage in innovative activities.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax-deductible proportion of R&D expenditure was officially increased.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly enhances the financial incentives for firms to invest in R&D, which is crucial for fostering innovation in the private sector.
reforms.source_quote: Tax incentives, which were previously very limited, have been increased, by bringing the tax-deductible proportion of R&D expenditure for companies from 25% to 100% in 2017.

reforms.description: The Slovak government launched a reform to improve the quality assurance system in public-sector research and align it with international standards.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the quality of public research is expected to lead to better research outputs and increased innovation capacity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government launched an ambitious reform aiming to address these shortcomings.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the public research quality assurance system, which is critical for improving research outputs and enhancing Slovakia's innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The government recently launched an ambitious reform aiming to address these shortcomings and align the quality assurance system with international standards.

reforms.description: The Slovak government launched the 'Once is Enough' initiative to reduce red tape by ensuring that individuals and enterprises do not need to submit documents already held by the State, effective from July 2018.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By streamlining administrative processes, the initiative reduces the burden on businesses, encouraging innovation and investment.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative was launched and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant step towards reducing bureaucratic hurdles, which can enhance the business environment and stimulate innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government launched the 'Once is Enough' initiative, which uses links between the administration’s different databases to ensure that... individuals and enterprises are no longer required to submit printouts of any documents already in the State’s possession.

reforms.description: In April 2018, Slovakia introduced a new system of investment aid aimed at encouraging the development of higher value-added activities, including research centres, with eligibility criteria focusing on average salaries rather than job creation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform incentivizes investment in high-tech sectors, which can lead to increased productivity and innovation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: A new system of investment aid entered into force designed to encourage the development of higher value added activities.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it shifts the focus of investment aid towards higher value-added activities, which can enhance the overall innovation capacity of the economy.
reforms.source_quote: In April 2018, a new system of investment aid entered into force designed to encourage the development of higher value added activities.

reforms.description: The Slovak Republic aims to promote research collaboration with innovative companies by including it in the assessment criteria for universities and public research institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between academia and industry is expected to drive innovation and improve the practical application of research.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is part of the recommendations included in the 2018 Slovak National Reform Programme.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to strengthen the link between research institutions and innovative companies, which is crucial for enhancing the innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Promote research and innovation by including research collaboration with innovative companies in the assessment of universities and public research institutions.

reforms.description: The Slovak Republic plans to provide direct support for centres of competence to enhance public-private collaboration in research and innovation, particularly in the automotive sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Direct support for centres of competence is expected to foster collaboration between public research institutions and private companies, leading to increased innovation and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is part of the recommendations included in the 2018 Slovak National Reform Programme.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance collaboration in a key sector, which is vital for the country's economic development.
reforms.source_quote: Consider providing direct support for centres of competence to strengthen public-private collaboration for research and innovation in areas such as the automotive industry.
